title: AIMS evidence bundle
description: Schema reference for validate_evidence.py. Validation is implemented in Python rather than a JSON Schema library to avoid runtime dependencies. Evidence bundles are the only material the AI qualitative layer (#92) may cite from.

notes.id: Stable evidence ID: 'ev-' + first 16 hex chars of SHA-256 over '<url>|<published_at>'. IDs are unique within a bundle; qualitative citations (#92) must reference these IDs.
notes.generated_at: Analysis-date midnight UTC, mirroring the analysis artifact; never wall-clock time.
notes.retrieved_at: Wall-clock UTC timestamp of the fetch run, recorded for provenance. Injectable for reproducible tests.
notes.coverage.sources: Per-source fetch outcomes keyed by 'yfinance:<symbol>' or 'feed:<source_id>': {status: success|failed, item_count?|error?}. A dead feed shows up here instead of silently shrinking the bundle.
notes.coverage.asset_classes: Per-asset-class direct-news coverage: {instruments, with_direct_news}. Equity rows get rich per-symbol news; indices and commodities rely mostly on macro feeds — the asymmetry is recorded so the #92 prompt can be honest about it.
notes.canonical_ids: Instrument tags via canonical_id from canonical_instrument_mappings.csv. Empty for macro items, which tag asset_classes instead.
notes.text_caps: title <= 300 chars; snippet <= 500 chars; markup stripped. Fetched text is untrusted data and is never interpreted as instructions.
notes.item_caps: <= 5 items per instrument (newest first), <= 10 items per macro source; items within [analysis_date - lookback_days, analysis_date + 1 day]; deduplicated by evidence ID across sources.
notes.sources_file: Curated macro feed list lives in data/mappings/evidence_sources.csv (source_id, name, url, category, pipe-separated asset_classes). See OPERATIONS.md for how to add a feed and the retention policy.
